_note: SnapCal 'Eat Out' elite feature data. Macros sourced from official chain nutrition info (2025-2026) cross-checked vs Nutritionix. Goal keys match the app: lose_weight | maintain | build_muscle (glp1 users => use lose_weight picks, smallest portions).

restaurants.verdict: Doable with intention — anchor on a lean protein (Egg White Delight, plain Egg McMuffin, McDouble, Quarter Pounder) and swap fries for fruit or a salad.

lose_weight.why: Lowest-cal hot breakfast with real protein; egg whites + lean Canadian bacon keep you full under 300 cal.

lose_weight.name: 6-pc Chicken McNuggets (no fries, sauce on side)
lose_weight.why: Portion-controlled protein you can eat in the car; skip fries and a large soda.

maintain.name: Egg McMuffin
maintain.why: The classic balanced breakfast: whole egg, Canadian bacon, English muffin — no sausage fat bomb.

maintain.name: McDouble (no extra cheese)
maintain.why: High protein-per-calorie; a single McDouble is a reasonable meal anchor.

build_muscle.name: Quarter Pounder with Cheese
build_muscle.why: 30g protein in one sandwich — an efficient muscle-building anchor.

order_hacks: Choose Egg White Delight or a plain Egg McMuffin over anything with sausage — sausage adds ~15g fat for little extra protein.

treat.name: Crispy Rice Bowl
treat.why: Crispy-rice texture and sauce scratch the takeout itch while still landing 31g protein and staying under the Harvest Bowl in fat.

restaurants.verdict: CAVA is one of the easiest places to eat genuinely healthy because the proteins are lean and you control every gram; the only landmines are falafel, full-fat dressings, and stacking three or more dips. Anchor strategy: SuperGreens base + a double lean protein, then spend remaining calories on one grain OR two dips.

lose_weight.name: SuperGreens + Grilled Chicken + Tzatziki
lose_weight.why: Leanest real meal at CAVA — 33g protein on greens with no grain for ~325 cal.

lose_weight.name: SuperGreens + Grilled Chicken + Roasted Veg + Tzatziki
lose_weight.why: Veg and lean chicken on greens, no grain — filling and fiber-rich under 450.

maintain.name: Tahini Caesar Bowl
maintain.why: Official composed bowl — balanced 37g protein on a greens base.

maintain.name: SuperGreens + Harissa Honey Chicken + Brown Rice + Hummus
maintain.why: Balanced grain bowl built on the fan-favorite sweet-spicy chicken.

build_muscle.name: Chicken + RightRice Bowl
build_muscle.why: Official bowl — 46g protein, and RightRice doubles as a high-protein grain.

build_muscle.name: Black Lentils + Double Grilled Chicken + SuperGreens + Tzatziki
build_muscle.why: Roughly 80g protein — the highest-protein build at CAVA, with lentils adding 17g.

order_hacks: Double the protein — chicken is 250 cal / 28g and it's the biggest single upgrade to any bowl's macros.
Base on SuperGreens (40 cal) instead of rice (290-370 cal) to bank 250+ cal for protein and dips.
Black Lentils is the smartest 'grain' — 17g protein and 15g fiber for 260 cal vs basmati's 6g protein.
Skip Greek Vinaigrette (14g fat); use Yogurt Dill or Lemon-Herb Tahini and add a spread for ~35-45 cal.

treat.name: Spicy Chicken + Avocado Pita
treat.why: The warm pita with spicy chicken and avocado is the most crave-worthy thing on the menu, and at least brings 50g protein to the splurge.

restaurants.verdict: Qdoba is very controllable and uniquely lets you stack free guac plus multiple free salsas while subbing in cauliflower or lettuce; the only real traps are the tortilla, queso, and double rice. Anchor strategy: double grilled chicken on cauliflower-mash or lettuce, load free veggies, salsa and guac, skip the tortilla and queso.

lose_weight.name: Keto Bowl - Chicken
lose_weight.why: Official low-carb bowl — 33g protein and only 11g carbs on a cauliflower base.

lose_weight.name: Chicken + Black Beans + Fajita Veggies + Pico + Salsa Verde (no rice)
lose_weight.why: Double-fiber and lean — 38g protein under 360 cal with no rice.

maintain.name: Chicken Bowl: Cilantro Lime Rice + Black Beans + Pico + Guac
maintain.why: The classic balanced burrito bowl with free guac.

maintain.name: Citrus Lime Chicken Salad
maintain.why: Official composed salad — 35g protein on a greens base.

build_muscle.name: Double Protein Bowl - Chicken
build_muscle.why: Highest protein on the menu — 59g in one official bowl.

build_muscle.name: Chicken + Brown Rice + Black Beans + Shredded Cheese + Pico
build_muscle.why: 43g protein with brown rice and beans for a clean muscle-build bowl.

order_hacks: Get a bowl, not a burrito — the 12.5" flour tortilla alone is 300 cal / 7g fat / 52g carbs.
Swap rice for Cauliflower Mash or lettuce to cut ~150 cal and ~37g carbs while keeping the bowl full.
Guac is free on entrees — take it; the queso and sour-cream combo adds more fat for less benefit.
Double the chicken: each 3.5oz scoop is +170 cal and +23g protein with only 9g fat.

treat.name: Chicken Queso Bowl
treat.why: Loaded queso indulgence that still delivers 47g protein, making it the least-damaging 'loaded' order.

restaurants.verdict: El Pollo Loco is one of the easiest fast-food chains to eat healthy at because flame-grilled chicken IS the identity. Anchor strategy: make the skinless grilled breast (180 cal / 35g protein / 0 carb) your base, add black beans and fresh veggies, and dial back rice, cheese, sour cream and creamy dressings.

lose_weight.name: Double Chicken Avocado Salad (no dressing)
lose_weight.why: Best protein-per-calorie on the menu; only 14g carbs without the dressing.

lose_weight.name: Chicken Tostada Salad (no shell, no dressing)
lose_weight.why: Big, filling chicken-and-veggie salad once you skip the fried shell and creamy dressing.

maintain.name: Chicken Black Bean Bowl (Pollo Fit)
maintain.why: Balanced one-bowl meal with only 11g fat and high fiber.

maintain.name: Original Pollo Bowl
maintain.why: Classic rice-beans-chicken bowl that keeps you full at moderate calories.

build_muscle.name: 3pc Flame-Grilled Chicken (skinless, a la carte)
build_muscle.why: Pure protein anchor — 60g+ protein and near-zero carbs; pull the skin to cut fat.

build_muscle.name: Double Chicken Avocado Salad (no dressing)
build_muscle.why: 48g protein for 370 cal — the best muscle ratio on the menu.

smart_swaps.instead_of: Classic Tostada Salad, fried shell + dressing (830 cal)
smart_swaps.get: Tostada Salad, no shell, no dressing (430 cal)

smart_swaps.instead_of: Double Chicken Avocado Salad WITH dressing (530 cal)

order_hacks: Order chicken a la carte and ask for it skinless — drops the breast from ~220 to 180 cal, keeps all 35g protein.
On any salad, ask for no fried shell and dressing on the side — that's where 300-400 cal and most of the fat hide.
Default to black beans over pinto (140 cal / 8g protein vs 200 / 11g) — saves ~60 cal and is nearly fat-free.
Build a Pollo Fit bowl: double chicken + black beans + fresh veggies, light or no rice, skip sour cream and cheese.

treat.name: Original Pollo Bowl
treat.why: The crave-worthy rice-beans-cheese-salsa comfort bowl that's still grilled-chicken-based and only 12g fat — the most satisfying splurge that won't blow the day.

restaurants.verdict: Tim Hortons is easy to keep healthy if you respect the cup — the coffee is fine; the cream, sugar and pastries are the damage. Anchor strategy: black or Americano coffee (or a small skim latte) plus an egg-and-cheese English muffin keeps you at 270-440 cal with real protein before any pastry tempts you.

lose_weight.name: Plain Homestyle Oatmeal (small) + black coffee
lose_weight.why: High-fiber, near-zero-fat warm breakfast; black coffee adds 0-5 cal.

lose_weight.name: Egg & Cheese English Muffin
lose_weight.why: Lowest-cal real egg breakfast sandwich with solid protein on a portion-controlled muffin.

maintain.name: Bacon, Egg & Cheese English Muffin
maintain.why: Balanced classic; bacon adds flavor and protein without going overboard.

maintain.name: Greek Yogurt w/ Berries & Granola + small 2% Latte
maintain.why: Protein-and-fruit combo that eats like a full breakfast.

build_muscle.name: Egg & Cheese English Muffin + small Protein Latte
build_muscle.why: About 33g protein under 450 cal — the chain's best high-protein combo.

build_muscle.name: Bacon & Cheese Omelette Bites (2pc) + small Protein Latte
build_muscle.why: Highest protein-per-calorie play and low carb.

smart_swaps.get: Bacon Egg & Cheese English Muffin (330 cal)
Coffee with a splash of milk, no sugar (~25 cal)

smart_swaps.instead_of: Boston Cream Donut as breakfast (230 cal)
smart_swaps.get: Greek Yogurt with berries & granola (250 cal)

order_hacks: Black coffee or Americano is ~0-5 cal; a medium Double-Double is 200+ cal — the cream and sugar are the problem, not the coffee.
Order lattes small with skim or 2% milk; the milk is your protein (9-17g) — skip flavor syrups (each pump is ~50 cal).
Choose an English muffin base over a bagel, biscuit, or croissant — saves ~120-200 cal and a lot of fat.
Pick egg-white or omelette-bite options for protein with the least fat, and ask for no hash brown in combos.

treat.name: Boston Cream Donut
treat.why: The iconic custard-filled, chocolate-topped classic — one of the lower-fat donuts and far lighter than a filled-and-frosted Dream donut or an Iced Capp.

restaurants.verdict: Smoothie King is a sugar trap disguised as health food — many 'fitness' blends top 600-1000 cal with 50g+ sugar at large sizes. Anchor strategy: order the smallest 20oz size, build on the Gladiator line for muscle (45-46g protein, ~1g sugar) or Slim-N-Trim for weight loss, and cut added sweeteners.

lose_weight.name: Slim-N-Trim Vanilla (20oz)
lose_weight.why: Lightest real smoothie; 14g protein, low fat, stevia-sweetened.

lose_weight.name: Slim-N-Trim Veggie (20oz)
lose_weight.why: Veggie-blend Slim option with high protein-to-calorie and filling fiber.

maintain.name: Slim-N-Trim Veggie (20oz)
maintain.why: Balanced and light enough to leave room in the day.

maintain.name: Gladiator Chocolate + half banana add-in (20oz)
maintain.why: Builds the lean Gladiator base into a fuller, balanced meal.

build_muscle.name: Gladiator Chocolate (20oz)
build_muscle.why: 46g protein and 1g sugar — the cleanest muscle smoothie on the menu.

build_muscle.name: Gladiator Vanilla (20oz)
build_muscle.why: Same lean high-protein base, unsweetened and endlessly customizable.

order_hacks: Always order the 20oz — Smoothie King defaults to big sizes; 32/40oz can nearly double calories AND sugar.
Ask them to 'make it skinny' and hold the added turbinado sugar or honey many blends include.
Swap to almond milk or a water base to cut calories and saturated fat vs juice or sherbet bases.
Start from the Gladiator unsweetened high-protein base and add half a banana or berries instead of a sugar-loaded blend.

treat.name: Peanut Power Plus (20oz)
treat.why: Rich peanut-butter-banana shake that tastes like dessert but still delivers 20g protein and zero added sugar — just order the 20oz, not the 40.

restaurants.verdict: You can eat reasonably at Cane's because the fingers themselves are a solid 130 cal / 13g protein each — the damage is entirely in the sauce, toast, fries, and lemonade. Anchor strategy: plain fingers a la carte plus water or unsweet tea turns a 1,250-cal combo into a 400-520 cal protein plate.

lose_weight.name: 2 Chicken Fingers + Coleslaw, no sauce
lose_weight.why: Smallest realistic combo; slaw adds bulk for far fewer calories than fries.

lose_weight.name: 3 Chicken Fingers, no sauce
lose_weight.why: Pure protein anchor — fingers only, sauce skipped saves ~190 cal of fat.

maintain.name: 4 Chicken Fingers, no sauce
maintain.why: A full Box's worth of protein without the fries, toast and sauce that double the calories.

maintain.name: 3 Chicken Fingers + Coleslaw + 1 dipping sauce
maintain.why: Balanced ~600-cal plate; one small sauce instead of the full cup keeps fat in check.

build_muscle.name: 6 Chicken Fingers (Caniac), no sauce/fries
build_muscle.why: Highest-protein order on the menu — strip the combo sides and it's nearly 80g protein.

build_muscle.name: 4 Chicken Fingers + 1 sauce + Texas Toast
build_muscle.why: High protein plus carbs from toast to refuel after training; skip the fries.

smart_swaps.get: 4 Chicken Fingers a la carte (520 cal)
Skip it or dip light in BBQ (~45 cal)

order_hacks: Order fingers a la carte (or a Caniac but bag the fries) — the combo's fries, toast and sauce add ~700 cal.
Skip the Cane's Sauce: -190 cal and -19g fat for the cup, the single biggest hidden fat bomb here.
Ditch the Texas toast (-140 cal, butter-soaked).
Swap fries for coleslaw (-290 cal) and get unsweet tea or water, not lemonade (-180 cal).

treat.name: The Sandwich (3 fingers, sauce, bun)
treat.why: The crave-worthy Cane's experience, sauce and all, but it still delivers 48g protein — far better than a Box Combo for the same indulgence.

restaurants.verdict: Zaxby's is one of the easier fried-chicken stops to eat clean because the grilled Zalad and grilled sandwich are genuinely lean — the whole game is grilled-not-fried and Lite Vinaigrette-not-Ranch. Anchor strategy: a Grilled House Zalad with dressing on the side lands at ~400 cal and 46g protein.

lose_weight.name: Garden House Zalad (no chicken) + Lite Vinaigrette
lose_weight.why: Lightest real meal; add grilled chicken for ~150 cal more if you want more protein.

lose_weight.name: Grilled House Zalad, no toast, Lite Vinaigrette on side
lose_weight.why: Grilled chicken over greens with the 30-cal vinaigrette — highest protein-per-calorie on the menu.

maintain.name: Grilled Chicken Sandwich (no fries)
maintain.why: One handheld, balanced at ~470 cal with 38g protein when you skip the combo fries and drink.

maintain.name: Grilled House Zalad with toast + Lite Vinaigrette
maintain.why: Full salad with the toast left on, still under 600 cal when you avoid ranch.

build_muscle.name: Grilled Zalad + extra grilled chicken, Lite Vinaigrette
build_muscle.why: About 60g protein and low carb — double the grilled fillet and keep the dressing light.

build_muscle.name: 5 Chicken Fingerz, not sauced (no fries/toast)
build_muscle.why: 46g protein in one order; fried but the highest-protein non-salad option if you skip the sides.

smart_swaps.get: Grilled Chicken Sandwich, no fries (470 cal)

order_hacks: Get GRILLED — the grilled sandwich (470 cal) and grilled Zalad beat anything fried by 200-400 cal.
Ask for dressing on the side and pick Lite Vinaigrette (30 cal) over Ranch (190-200 cal) — the biggest single save on a salad.
Drop the Texas toast that comes on Zalads and plates (-~150 cal).
Go light on Zax Sauce (180 cal / 18g fat) and sub a side salad for crinkle fries.

treat.name: 5 Chicken Fingerz with Zax Sauce
treat.why: The signature Zaxby's fix — hand-breaded fingerz with Zax Sauce — and it still packs 47g protein, so it's the least-damaging way to indulge if you bag the fries and toast.

restaurants.verdict: Bojangles is very doable healthy because the Grilled Chicken Salad (360 cal / 40g protein) and grilled sandwich are legitimately lean, and the green beans and Cajun pintos are some of the best low-cal sides in fast food. Anchor strategy: grilled chicken + a bean or veg side, and save the Bo-Berry biscuit for a true cheat day.

lose_weight.name: 3 Chicken Supremes + Green Beans
lose_weight.why: Fewest tenders plus the near-zero-cal veg side instead of a biscuit or fries.

lose_weight.name: Grilled Chicken Salad, light dressing on side
lose_weight.why: 40g protein and only 9g carbs — the leanest high-protein meal in the building.

maintain.name: Grilled Chicken Sandwich (no mayo)
maintain.why: Balanced handheld at ~470 cal; ask for no mayo to shave fat further.

maintain.name: 4 Chicken Supremes + Cajun Pintos
maintain.why: Protein-forward plate where the pintos (110 cal, 7g protein) replace the biscuit or fries.

build_muscle.name: Grilled Chicken Salad + 3 Chicken Supremes
build_muscle.why: Stacks the 40g-protein salad with supremes for ~63g protein, still low-ish carb.

build_muscle.name: 4 Chicken Supremes + Cajun Pintos + Green Beans
build_muscle.why: 40g protein with two fiber-rich bean and veg sides instead of one starchy biscuit.

order_hacks: Go grilled, not fried — the Grilled Chicken Sandwich (470 cal) and Grilled Chicken Salad (360 cal) beat the fried filet by 150-200+ cal.
Pick a lean side: Green Beans (~25 cal) or Cajun Pintos (110 cal) over Dirty Rice (170), Mac & Cheese, or fries.
Skip the Bo-Berry Biscuit as a 'side' — it's a 370-cal dessert (18g sugar, 17g fat) hiding in plain sight.
Get salad dressing on the side and use half; ask for no mayo on grilled sandwiches.

treat.name: Bo-Berry Biscuit
treat.why: The iconic blueberry-studded, glazed Bojangles crave — a genuine dessert, but at 370 cal it's the most reasonable single splurge if you anchor the meal on grilled chicken.

restaurants.verdict: Wingstop is one of the easier indulgent chains to eat well at because classic wings are essentially pure protein at 0g carbs — the calories live almost entirely in the fries, the dips and the creamy sauces. Anchor strategy: 6-10 classic bone-in wings with a dry rub or thin wet sauce, no fries, no ranch.

lose_weight.name: 6 Classic Plain Wings (no fries, no dip)
lose_weight.why: Pure protein, zero carbs — the leanest real order once you skip the fries and ranch.

lose_weight.name: 8 Original Hot Classic Wings (no sides)
lose_weight.why: Original Hot is a thin wet sauce, so you get heat and flavor with barely any added calories over plain.

maintain.name: 10 Classic Plain Wings + celery (no dip)
maintain.why: Big satisfying portion around 510 cal with a massive 100g protein hit; celery adds crunch for almost nothing.

maintain.name: 6 Lemon Pepper Classic Wings + carrots
maintain.why: Lemon Pepper is a dry rub (1g carb) so it stays balanced, paired with a veg side instead of fries.

build_muscle.name: 10 Classic Plain Wings
build_muscle.why: 100g protein and zero carbs — about as protein-dense as fast food gets.

build_muscle.name: 10 Original Hot Classic Wings
build_muscle.why: Three-quarters of your day's protein in one order with a wet sauce that adds almost no calories.

smart_swaps.get: Wings + extra dry rub or celery (~0 cal)

smart_swaps.instead_of: Garlic Parmesan wings + Seasoned Fries (+390 cal)

order_hacks: Order CLASSIC bone-in, not boneless — boneless are breaded nuggets (more carbs, less protein); classic are real wings at 0g carbs.
Pick dry rubs or thin wet sauces (Lemon Pepper, Original Hot, Louisiana Rub) over creamy ones to add ~0g carb vs ranch or parm.
Skip the dips or use the rub for flavor — one Ranch cup (313 cal, 33g fat) can out-calorie six plain wings.
Swap fries for veggie sticks: carrots and celery are 5-17 cal vs ~390 cal for regular seasoned fries.

treat.name: 8 Garlic Parmesan Classic Wings
treat.why: Rich, buttery, cheesy and crave-worthy — but still 64g protein and only 4g carbs, so it's the indulgent pick that does the least damage to your macros.

restaurants.verdict: Shake Shack is calorie-dense but workable: the patties are quality protein and a single burger fits almost any goal — the trouble is the fries-plus-shake combo. Anchor strategy: a single burger (or grilled chicken), skip or split the shake, and treat fries as a shared side.

lose_weight.name: Single Hamburger (no cheese)
lose_weight.why: The lowest-calorie real burger on the menu — one Angus patty on a potato bun with 25g protein.

lose_weight.name: Single Cheeseburger
lose_weight.why: Adds cheese and 4g protein for only 70 more calories — still a sensible single under 500.

maintain.name: Single ShackBurger
maintain.why: The classic with ShackSauce and lettuce/tomato lands right in the 500-cal sweet spot with 29g protein.

maintain.name: Single Cheeseburger + split a small fry
maintain.why: Keep the burger as the anchor and share fries so you stay near 600 instead of solo-ing a 470-cal fry.

build_muscle.name: Double ShackBurger
build_muscle.why: Two patties deliver 51g protein in one sandwich — the highest-protein single item on the menu.

build_muscle.name: Chick'n Shack (grilled if available)
build_muscle.why: 33g protein from a chicken breast; ask for it grilled where offered to cut the fry-batter fat.

smart_swaps.get: ShackBurger only, water or diet soda

order_hacks: Order a SINGLE, not a double — a single ShackBurger (500) vs double (790) is the biggest lever on the menu.
Ask for it lettuce-wrapped / no bun to drop ~24g carbs and ~120 cal while keeping all the protein.
Skip or SPLIT the shake — a Vanilla Shake (680 cal) can have more calories than the burger itself.
Choose plain crinkle fries over cheese fries (470 vs 710), or share one order across the table.

treat.name: Vanilla Hand-Spun Shake
treat.why: A real frozen-custard shake — thick, rich and the thing Shake Shack is famous for; 18g protein softens the blow, and splitting it halves the damage to ~340 cal.

restaurants.verdict: DQ is two restaurants in one: the grilled chicken sandwich (390 cal, 29g protein) is a genuinely solid pick, while the Blizzards are dessert-as-a-meal calorie bombs. Anchor strategy: grilled chicken or a single cheeseburger, and if you want the famous Blizzard, order it MINI.

lose_weight.name: Grilled Chicken Sandwich (no mayo)
lose_weight.why: Best protein-to-calorie ratio on the savory menu — 29g protein under 400 cal; no mayo trims more fat.

lose_weight.name: Small Vanilla Cone
lose_weight.why: The lowest-calorie way to actually get DQ soft-serve — a portion-controlled cone instead of a 600+ cal Blizzard.

maintain.name: Original Cheeseburger
maintain.why: A single classic cheeseburger is a balanced ~400-cal anchor with 22g protein.

maintain.name: Grilled Chicken Sandwich + Side Salad
maintain.why: Adds volume and greens to the leanest sandwich while staying in the maintenance band.

build_muscle.name: 4-Piece Chicken Strip Basket
build_muscle.why: Highest single-order protein (35g) plus carbs to refuel — share or skip the toast and gravy to cut it down.

build_muscle.name: Grilled Chicken Sandwich + Original Cheeseburger
build_muscle.why: Stacking the two leanest mains gets you 51g protein from real meat without the deep fryer.

smart_swaps.get: Mini Choc Chip Cookie Dough Blizzard (420 cal)

order_hacks: Get the GRILLED Chicken Sandwich, never the crispy/breaded — grilled saves roughly 150-250 cal and a load of fat.
Size DOWN on frozen treats: a MINI Blizzard (420) vs a medium (700-1000+) is the single biggest save at DQ.
A small Vanilla Cone (220) or a No-Sugar-Added Fudge Bar (50 cal, 4g protein) scratches the ice-cream itch for a fraction of a Blizzard.
Skip the fries-and-toast that pad the Chicken Strip Basket to 1020 cal — order strips a la carte for the protein only.

treat.name: Mini Chocolate Chip Cookie Dough Blizzard
treat.why: The iconic DQ Blizzard — cookie dough chunks in soft-serve — in the mini size, so you get the full crave-worthy experience at ~420 cal instead of 700-1000+ for a medium.

restaurants.verdict: You can absolutely eat clean at Wawa — the build-your-own hoagie line is your friend and the kiosk lets you customize everything. Anchor strategy: build a cold turkey or grilled-chicken hoagie on a Shorti or wheat roll, order it 'no mayo, extra veggies, mustard or light dressing,' and an 800-cal bomb becomes a 350-450 cal lean meal.

lose_weight.name: Junior Oven Roasted Turkey Hoagie (no mayo)
lose_weight.why: Portion-controlled hoagie — lean turkey, small roll, loads of veggies. Filling and protein-forward for the calories.

lose_weight.name: Cold Turkey Shorti on Wheat (cucumbers, spinach, no mayo)
lose_weight.why: Wheat roll adds fiber, veggies add volume, skipping mayo cuts ~150 cal of pure fat. Satisfying lunch under 400.

maintain.name: Mediterranean Grilled Chicken Salad
maintain.why: Grilled chicken, romaine, tomato, cucumber, feta, olives — high protein, low carb, balanced fat. Get dressing on the side.

maintain.name: Cold Oven Roasted Turkey Hoagie on Shorti (lettuce/pickles, light mayo)
maintain.why: Classic everyday order in the maintenance sweet spot — solid protein, real bread, keeps you full till dinner.

build_muscle.name: Custom Grilled Chicken Salad (double chicken)
build_muscle.why: 51g protein at only 6g carbs — elite muscle-building macros. Go light on cheese/dressing to trim the fat.

build_muscle.name: Classic Oven Roasted Turkey Hoagie (10-inch, no mayo)
build_muscle.why: 52g protein plus carbs to refuel a hard training day. No-mayo keeps the fat reasonable for the protein you get.

smart_swaps.get: Classic Oven Roasted Turkey Hoagie, no mayo (700 cal)

smart_swaps.instead_of: Custom Grilled Chicken Salad, full mayo/cheese (590 cal)
smart_swaps.get: Mediterranean Grilled Chicken Salad, light dressing (400 cal)

smart_swaps.instead_of: Classic Honey Smoked Turkey Hoagie w/ mayo (890 cal)
smart_swaps.get: Cold Turkey Shorti on Wheat, no mayo (380 cal)

order_hacks: Order any hoagie 'no mayo, extra veggies, mustard or light Italian' — mayo alone is ~100-200 cal of straight fat per sandwich.
Default to the Shorti (6") or Junior over the Classic (10") roll — same flavors, roughly half the bread calories.
Pick the wheat roll for fiber, or order it as a salad/bowl (no bread) to drop ~200 carb-calories entirely.
Choose lean proteins — oven-roasted or honey-smoked turkey and grilled chicken — over Italian meats, meatball, or cheesesteak, which can double the calories and fat.

treat.name: Shorti Cheesesteak (no extra cheese sauce)
treat.why: The crave-worthy Wawa cheesesteak hit for ~490 cal vs a 970-cal meatball or 890-cal honey-smoked bomb — gooey, savory, real protein, and roughly half the damage.

restaurants.verdict: Sheetz is very doable thanks to its 'Eat Shmart' line and fully customizable MTO touchscreen — you control every topping and sauce. Anchor strategy: build a turkey or grilled-chicken sub on wheat (or skip the bread for an MTO Bowl), choose egg whites at breakfast, and swap mayo for mustard or fat-free Italian.

lose_weight.name: 6" Turkey Sub on Wheat (mustard, veggies, no mayo)
lose_weight.why: Lean turkey, light bread, no mayo — only 4g fat and 22g protein. One of the most filling sub-400 picks on the board.

lose_weight.name: Grilled Chicken Salad, fat-free Italian
lose_weight.why: 32g protein for ~310 cal is excellent satiety per calorie. Skip the croutons to trim further.

maintain.name: Grilled Chicken Salad, full toppings + dressing
maintain.why: 35g protein, balanced carbs and fat, lots of veggie volume — a complete meal right in the maintenance zone.

maintain.name: 6" Turkey Sub (standard build, light mayo)
maintain.why: The everyday MTO order — solid protein and real bread in the 500-cal range; drop to light/no mayo to push it lower.

build_muscle.name: MTO Bowl — Grilled Chicken over rice, extra protein (no bread)
build_muscle.why: Skipping the roll for a bowl saves ~200 carb-calories while loading double grilled chicken for recovery.

build_muscle.name: Grilled Chicken Salad + extra grilled chicken
build_muscle.why: Doubling the chicken pushes protein near 48g while keeping carbs low — a lean-bulk-friendly plate.

smart_swaps.instead_of: Sausage Shmuffin w/ egg & cheese (490 cal)
smart_swaps.get: Turkey Sausage, Egg White & Cheese Shmuffin (~280 cal)
6" Turkey Sub on Wheat, mustard/no mayo (320 cal)

smart_swaps.instead_of: Grilled Chicken Salad w/ ranch + croutons (~450 cal)
smart_swaps.get: Grilled Chicken Salad w/ fat-free Italian (311 cal)

order_hacks: On any breakfast Shmuffin or flatbread, choose egg whites and turkey sausage instead of sausage/bacon — saves ~150-200 cal and a lot of fat.
Order subs on wheat with mustard, banana peppers, and extra veggies instead of mayo — mayo is the single biggest calorie add on an MTO sub.
Build it as an MTO Bowl (no bread) to cut ~200 carb-calories while keeping all the protein and toppings.
Default to grilled chicken or turkey over fried chicken, steak, or the loaded melts — and ask for fat-free Italian on salads.

treat.name: Ham Shmuffin with Egg & Cheese
treat.why: That warm, melty breakfast-sandwich craving for just 230 cal — less than half of a 490-cal Sausage Shmuffin and a fraction of the loaded burrito bombs. The least-damaging way to scratch the itch.

restaurants.verdict: Royal Farms is a fried-chicken destination first, so the honest play is to anchor on a single lean protein build (a couple of tenders or a turkey/grilled-style sub) and skip the western-fries-plus-large-soda combo that turns a 400-cal order into a 1,000+ bomb. Treat the famous fried chicken as the indulgence it is, eat it without the fried sides, and you can eat reasonably here.

lose_weight.name: 2 Classic Chicken Tenders (à la carte, no fries/roll)
lose_weight.why: Two tenders ordered alone (not as the meal) is the most protein-dense low-cal chicken option; skip the fries and roll that turn the meal into 710 cal.

lose_weight.name: Fried Chicken Leg (1 piece)
lose_weight.why: The smallest bone-in piece by calories; pair with a side salad or mashed potatoes instead of western fries for a sub-400-cal plate that still scratches the fried-chicken itch.

maintain.name: Chicken Tender Sub (whole)
maintain.why: A real built sub in the maintain band; ask for lettuce/tomato and light sauce rather than mayo-heavy to keep the fat in check.

maintain.name: Mac & Cheese (large) as a meal
maintain.why: Surprisingly balanced comfort side eaten as the main — solid protein for a meatless pick; stop here and skip the chicken add-on.

build_muscle.name: Spicy Buffalo Chicken Sub
build_muscle.why: Highest realistic protein-per-order on the sub board (38g) with carbs to refuel; runs leaner than the Classic RoFo Buffalo (880 cal).

build_muscle.name: Fried Chicken Breast (1 piece) + Mashed Potatoes (small)
build_muscle.why: One bone-in breast delivers 32g protein; mashed potatoes (not western fries) keep carbs moderate for a post-lift refuel. A muscle-build pick, not a cutting pick.

smart_swaps.instead_of: 3-Piece Tender Meal w/ fries & roll (920 cal)
smart_swaps.get: 2 Classic Tenders à la carte (380 cal)

order_hacks: Order chicken pieces or tenders À LA CARTE, not as a 'meal' — the meal adds western fries and a dinner roll that tack on 300-500 cal of fried starch.
Swap western fries (small 490 / large 730 cal) for mashed potatoes (small 180 cal) or a side salad — the biggest single calorie save on the menu.
On subs, ask for lettuce/tomato/onion and go light on mayo and creamy sauces — the sauce and bread, not the chicken, drive most of the fat.
Pick the leg or a single tender over the fried breast when you want the fried-chicken taste for the fewest calories; one breast (590 cal) is nearly 3x a leg (200 cal).

treat.name: Fried Chicken Breast (1 piece)
treat.why: Royal Farms' fried chicken is the whole reason to stop — this bone-in breast is a genuine indulgence, not everyday food. Solo (no large western fries, no soda) it's the least-damaging way to have the signature item: still 32g protein, and you avoid turning one piece into a 1,300-cal tray. Savor it on a cheat day and skip the fried sides.

restaurants.verdict: QT Kitchens is made-to-order, which is the healthy eater's advantage — you control the bread size, protein, and sauce, so a half-size sub on wheat with a lean meat is a genuinely solid 350-450 cal meal. Anchor on a HALF sub (the whole is essentially two meals) or a grilled-chicken item and skip the ranch-and-bacon flatbreads.

lose_weight.name: The Gardener Half Sub (wheat)
lose_weight.why: Lowest-calorie real sub on the board and veggie-forward; the half on wheat is filling for the calories. Add turkey or ham for more protein if you want.

lose_weight.name: Turkey Half Sub (wheat)
lose_weight.why: Leanest meat sub by fat (just 6g) at a low calorie count — order it loaded with veggies and mustard instead of mayo to keep it that lean.

maintain.name: Roast Beef Half Sub (wheat)
maintain.why: 30g protein for only 8g fat and 450 cal — one of the best protein-to-calorie ratios in the whole case, right in the maintain band.

maintain.name: Chicken & Cheese Flatbread
maintain.why: A balanced grab-and-go hot item; far leaner than the Chicken, Bacon & Ranch flatbread (570 cal) while still hot-pressed and satisfying.

build_muscle.name: Roast Beef Whole Sub (wheat)
build_muscle.why: 60g protein at only 15g fat — the standout muscle-build order in QT Kitchens. Doubles as two meals or one big post-lift refuel with clean macros.

build_muscle.name: Whole Wheat Turkey Sub
build_muscle.why: 47g protein for just 11g fat and 740 cal — exceptionally lean for the protein load, ideal to bulk without the fat of a meat-stack or BLT.

smart_swaps.instead_of: Double BLT Half Sub, wheat (540 cal)

order_hacks: Order a HALF sub, not a whole — a whole QT sub is essentially two meals (whole roast beef 900 cal vs. half 450 cal). Half on wheat is the default lean move.
Choose wheat bread and lean meats (turkey, ham, roast beef) — they run far lower in fat than the BLT, 3-Meat Stack, or Big Italian.
Skip mayo and creamy sauces; ask for mustard plus extra lettuce, tomato, onion, and peppers to bulk the sub without adding calories.
For breakfast, a single taco (chicken 310 cal) beats the burritos and scrambles — The Ultimate burrito is 900 cal.

treat.name: Chicken, Bacon & Ranch Flatbread
treat.why: The hot, melty bacon-ranch flatbread is QT's crave-worthy indulgence, and at 570 cal with 31g protein it's far less damaging than the cheat-day bombs (Ultimate burrito 900 cal, Double BLT whole sub 1,080 cal). Satisfies the salty-fatty craving while still delivering real protein.

restaurants.verdict: Yes, the pizza is the draw, but it's a calorie/fat trap by the slice-pile — eat healthy here by anchoring on a made-to-order sub built on lean meat (grilled chicken, turkey) instead of the pie. Order the sub, add a single slice if you must crave it, and you're in great shape.

lose_weight.name: Grilled Chicken Sub (regular)
lose_weight.why: Leanest hot sub on the board — high protein, only ~7g fat keeps you full for ~325 cal. The smartest single order at Casey's.

lose_weight.name: Ham, Egg & Cheese Croissant
lose_weight.why: Lowest-cal hot breakfast that still brings real protein; skip the sausage version (507 cal) and you save big.

maintain.name: Turkey & Cheese Sub (regular)
maintain.why: Balanced ~460 cal with a huge 35g protein hit and moderate fat — a textbook maintenance lunch.

maintain.name: Bacon, Egg & Cheese Croissant + a fruit/water side
maintain.why: Sits comfortably mid-range; pair with fruit/water to land in the 400-600 maintain window without a pizza spike.

build_muscle.name: Turkey & Cheese Sub (regular)
build_muscle.why: Highest verified protein single item (35g) for a reasonable calorie load — the muscle-builder's anchor order.

build_muscle.name: Grilled Chicken Sub + Ham & Cheese Classic add-on
build_muscle.why: Stack a 325-cal grilled chicken sub with the 230-cal/25g-protein Ham & Cheese Classic for ~47g protein at very low fat.

order_hacks: Anchor on a made-to-order sub with grilled chicken or turkey — it's leaner and far more protein-dense than pizza slices, which pile up fast at ~250-330 cal each.
If you crave pizza, buy ONE slice as a side to a sub — never order a personal/whole pie to eat solo (a few slices blows past 800-1000 cal).
At breakfast pick ham over sausage on the croissant (saves ~160 cal) and skip the hash-brown side; egg + lean ham still gives you ~17g protein.
Ask for light cheese / hold the oil & mayo on subs — that's where most of the fat hides; the lean meat and bread are the cheap part calorie-wise.

treat.name: One slice Large Pepperoni Pizza (1/12)
treat.why: Casey's signature crave fix without the damage — a single slice is ~266 cal with 13g protein, versus 800-1300 cal if you let a few slices pile up. Pair it with a side salad or grilled chicken sub and the indulgence is basically free.

restaurants.verdict: 7-Eleven is genuinely easy to eat healthy at if you ignore the roller grill and head to the cold case — hard-boiled eggs, fresh wraps/subs and salads are the play. Anchor on a high-protein cold pick (eggs, turkey, chicken Caesar no-dressing) and treat the taquitos as the splurge, not the meal.

lose_weight.name: Cage-Free Hard Boiled Eggs (2-pack)
lose_weight.why: Best low-cal/high-protein-per-calorie item in the store — 12g protein, zero carbs for 120 cal. Add fruit or string cheese for a complete light meal.

lose_weight.name: Chicken Caesar Salad (no dressing)
lose_weight.why: Skip the packet and it's a 190-cal, 17g-protein lunch; the dressing alone nearly triples it to ~390 cal, so keep it off or use half.

maintain.name: Southwest Turkey Sandwich
maintain.why: A filling grab-and-go sandwich at the top of the maintain band; pair with water and skip chips and it's a solid one-stop lunch.

maintain.name: Hard Boiled Eggs (2-pack) + Chicken Caesar Salad (no dressing)
maintain.why: Stacking two cold-case staples lands at ~310 cal with 29g protein — leaves room for fruit and still stays mid-range.

build_muscle.name: 2x Hard Boiled Eggs (4 eggs) + Chicken Caesar Salad (no dressing)
build_muscle.why: Two egg packs plus the lean salad clears ~41g protein for ~430 cal — the highest-protein realistic whole-food build at 7-Eleven.

build_muscle.name: Chicken Salad & Bacon Wrap
build_muscle.why: Single-item 20g protein with real carbs to refuel; calorie-dense but legit as a post-workout grab when you want one thing off the shelf.

smart_swaps.instead_of: Bacon & Cheese Egg Drop Sandwich (624 cal)
smart_swaps.get: Chicken Caesar Salad, no dressing (190 cal)

smart_swaps.instead_of: Chicken Caesar Salad WITH dressing (390 cal)
smart_swaps.get: Same salad, dressing on the side / skipped (190 cal)

order_hacks: Walk past the roller grill to the cold/fresh case — hard-boiled eggs, fresh wraps and salads are far more protein-dense per calorie than taquitos and rollers (180-300 cal each, mostly fat).
On any salad, get the dressing on the side and use half — the Caesar packet alone roughly doubles or triples the salad's calories (190 to 390 cal).
Build a DIY protein box: a 2-pack of hard-boiled eggs (120 cal/12g) plus string cheese or a fruit cup beats a packaged breakfast sandwich on protein-per-calorie.
If you want the roller grill, choose a leaner single taquito (Buffalo Chicken ~180 cal) over two cheesier ones, and pair it with eggs for protein instead of a second taquito.

treat.name: Monterey Jack & Chicken Taquito (single)
treat.why: The crave-worthy roller-grill classic for ~280 cal as a SINGLE — way less damage than a 624-cal egg-drop sandwich or a 550+ cal loaded wrap. One taquito + a 2-pack of eggs gives you the indulgence plus real protein.

restaurants.verdict: You can eat genuinely well at Cumberland Farms if you anchor on the made-to-order deli: build a turkey, ham, or grilled-chicken sub on the smaller/wheat bread, lean on the egg-white breakfast options, and treat the fried sides and footlongs as the calorie traps. Lean-protein sub or wrap + skip the chips/Chill Zone lands a 400-550 cal, 30g+ protein meal.

lose_weight.name: Veggie Burger
lose_weight.why: Lowest-fat hot item on the menu (2g) and high-fiber (5g) so it's filling for the calories; great when you want hot food without the grease.

lose_weight.name: Grilled Chicken, Bacon & Cheese
lose_weight.why: 39g protein for only 400 cal is the best protein-per-calorie ratio in the deli case; keeps you full for hours without a calorie blowout.

maintain.name: Turkey Monterey Jack White Sub
maintain.why: Balanced ~500 cal with 34g protein; a classic lean deli sub that hits maintenance macros cleanly.

maintain.name: Southwestern Turkey Wrap
maintain.why: ~490 cal, 31g protein and 5g fiber from the wrap and veggies; a well-rounded portable meal.

build_muscle.name: Deluxe Chicken Parmesan Sub
build_muscle.why: 43g protein for only 500 cal and 13g fat is the smartest high-protein build on the menu, far leaner than the footlong for the same protein.

build_muscle.name: Italian Footlong Sub
build_muscle.why: Highest single-item protein on the menu at 48g; a real bulking option after hard training, though the 36g fat and high sodium make it an occasional big-meal pick.

smart_swaps.get: Turkey Monterey Jack White Sub (500 cal)

order_hacks: Build subs on the white/wheat wedge or wrap rather than the footlong roll — the footlong nearly doubles calories and carbs for the same fillings.
Ask for extra lettuce, tomato, peppers and onions to bulk up the sandwich volume without adding calories, so it eats more filling.
Skip the Chill Zone frozen drinks (a 30oz is ~225 cal of pure sugar) and the fried sides (5-piece tenders are 550 cal); water or unsweetened coffee keeps the meal lean.
For breakfast, choose the Canadian Bacon, Egg Whites & Cheese English Muffin (220 cal, 13g protein) over a sausage/croissant build to save 150-350 calories.

treat.name: Egg & Sausage French Toast Sandwich
treat.why: Scratches the indulgent sweet-and-savory breakfast craving for 450 cal, and at 18g protein it's far less damaging than a 520-cal sausage croissant or a 590-cal raspberry turnover from the pastry case.

restaurants.verdict: Buc-ee's is a snack minefield — the famous Beaver Nuggets and fudge are pure sugar bombs — but the BBQ pit and grab-and-go cases are a legitimately strong place to eat lean if you anchor on smoked meat and jerky. Anchor strategy: chopped brisket or turkey from the deli plus high-protein fresh jerky and hard-boiled eggs, and you walk out fueled instead of sugar-crashed.

lose_weight.name: 2 Hard-Boiled Eggs + Fresh Fruit Cup
lose_weight.why: Eggs bring protein and fat to keep you full while the fruit cup adds fiber and volume; one of the only low-cal, whole-food combos in the building.

lose_weight.name: Peppered Turkey Jerky (1 oz)
lose_weight.why: Nearly pure protein with almost zero fat or carbs — the most protein-dense, lowest-calorie filling snack at Buc-ee's, ideal to kill hunger on the road.

maintain.name: Chopped Brisket Sandwich
maintain.why: The flagship lean anchor — real smoked brisket and 31g protein in a balanced ~550 cal sandwich; ask for light sauce to trim sugar.

maintain.name: Sausage & Cheese Kolache
maintain.why: A single kolache is a reasonable ~250-cal handheld; pair with jerky or an egg to round it into a balanced 400-500 cal meal.

build_muscle.name: Sliced BBQ Brisket Sandwich
build_muscle.why: Highest-protein hot item at ~38g from sliced brisket; the protein-dense choice when refueling after training.

build_muscle.name: Steakhouse Beef Jerky (2 oz)
build_muscle.why: 26g of clean protein in a portable 2-oz portion with minimal carbs — a high-protein anchor you can graze on.

smart_swaps.saves: -220 cal, real protein vs pure sugar

order_hacks: Hit the BBQ pit and grab-and-go case first, not the candy aisle — chopped brisket, turkey and hard-boiled eggs are the lean backbone of any Buc-ee's stop.
Buy ONE bag of jerky for protein and skip the Beaver Nuggets entirely; jerky is ~80-110 cal a serving of mostly protein vs the Nuggets' sugar-and-oil puff.
Ask for BBQ sauce on the side — the smoked meat is already flavorful and the sauce is where a lot of hidden sugar lives.
Grab a fresh fruit cup (~100 cal) as your 'treat' instead of the fudge slab, and you avoid a 200-470 cal sugar bomb.

treat.name: Sausage & Cheese Kolache
treat.why: The most satisfying crave-worthy Buc-ee's bite that won't wreck your day — warm, savory, only ~250 cal with 9g of actual protein, versus a ~470-cal double-fudge brownie or a giant fudge slab that's pure sugar. A small scoop of Beaver Nuggets (~140 cal/oz) is the runner-up if you want the iconic sweet.
